title: Rebuild 3: Gangs of Deadsville
notes: Each time the game is launched, it shows an in-game error message (that can be dismissed) which according to the developer's website, means that the game in unable to connect to/detect my graphics card... However, the game otherwise seems to work flawlessly.

I get the same mentioning that the video card drivers can't be found, and it is resorting to default (note: there's an ingame link to apparently fix it for linux, but it's leading nowhere). The game works, and can even continue cloud-saves. However: while it's not a quick reflexes game, mouse controls are severely slowed down compared to windows play.
title: Rebuild 3: Gangs of Deadsville

notes: I didn't get any graphics driver errors, and the menus seem to work fine, but the game itself is just a black screen.
title: Rebuild 3: Gangs of Deadsville

notes: It works just fine on my integrated GPU (I set graphics quality to Low).  On startup it displays the warning: "Warning: failed to detect GPU, running in sloooow software mode. Your video card driver may be out of date. More info and special instructions for Linux at: rebuildgame.com/help.php".  Not sure why it's displaying a message related to Linux when it's a Windows-only Steam Play game. Either it used to be a Linux game or they've tested it with Steam Play and added a special message.  But it works anyhow.
